pages.title: Resume
pages.description: Professional resume for Staff software engineer Wren Maddox.

resume.title: Staff Software Engineer · Distributed Systems
resume.summary: Staff engineer with 11+ years building the infrastructure that keeps high-traffic systems alive. I take ambiguous systems problems from design doc to production and leave them more boring than I found them.

points: Re-architected the stream-processing core, cutting p99 query latency from 1.8s to 240ms across a 4.2M events/sec firehose.
Introduced tiered hot/cold storage, trimming infrastructure spend by ~$3.1M/year with no SLA regressions.
Chair the reliability guild and authored the incident-review practice now used company-wide.
Technical mentor to six senior engineers; reviewer on every cross-team design doc.
Built the distributed trace-ingestion pipeline from scratch — later extracted and open-sourced as riffle (8.4k stars).
Designed the sharding model that scaled the platform from 4k to 60k customers without a rewrite.
Shipped adaptive alerting that cut overnight on-call pages by 70%.
First engineering hire. Built the ingestion backbone for satellite and ground-sensor data — ~12 TB/day.
Stood up the company's first data warehouse and the dashboards the science team still runs on.

projects.name: toy-raft
projects.description: A from-scratch Raft implementation built to actually understand leader election and log replication. The part that bit me was snapshotting and log compaction.

projects.name: plotterd
projects.description: A tiny columnar time-series store, written in Zig to get hands-on with manual memory management and a language I never touch at work.

projects.name: blog
projects.description: An ongoing web log working through distributed-systems papers in plain language, each with a small runnable example.
